title: cheilanthes marginata f. typica
intro: Cheilanthes marginata f. typica, also known as the "Marginated Ruff-sedge", is a species of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Cheilanthes marginata f. typica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
